seoTitle: Mount Templeton vs Mount Wedge — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Templeton and Mount Wedge. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Templeton vs Mount Wedge: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Templeton and Mount Wedge.
heading: Mount Templeton vs Mount Wedge — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Templeton, South Australia and Mount Wedge, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Templeton has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Mount Wedge (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Templeton or Mount Wedge safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Templeton has a flood risk score of 49/100, while Mount Wedge scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Templeton scores 24/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Wedge scores 46/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Templeton or Mount Wedge?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Templeton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
